rationale: The citing paper is an editorial synthesis that surveys prior multimedia/text research rather than using a single focal empirical source, and we only have a DOI-level match with no in-text citation context. The cited paper is topically relevant (online review text, deceptive cues, verified purchase linkage), so it could be used substantively in the review discussion, but the current evidence is too sparse to confirm importance. Given this mix, it is not safe to drop as an obvious mention-only citation.
